name: create-git-worktree
description: git worktree を利用した分離作業環境を自動構築します。デフォルトブランチから最新コードを取得し、.git-worktrees/ ディレクトリに新規worktreeを作成、.env・npm依存関係を自動セットアップします。ブランチ名の '/' は自動的に '-' に変換されます。既存worktreeは再利用されます。
capabilities: Creates isolated git worktrees in a dedicated directory
Automatically converts branch names with slashes to hyphens
Reuses existing worktrees to avoid redundant setup
Copies .env files to the new worktree
Pulls latest changes from base branch before creating worktree
Shows clear next steps after worktree creation
author: AKABANAKK
securitySummary: Benign git worktree automation script. Creates isolated development environments using standard git commands. Copies .env for local development setup, which is documented expected behavior. No network calls, no credential exfiltration patterns, no obfuscation detected. All static findings are false positives from legitimate git automation patterns.
valueStatement: Managing multiple branches in the same repository can lead to context switching issues and workspace clutter. This skill automates the creation of isolated git worktrees in a dedicated directory, copying environment files and setting up clean workspaces for each feature branch.
limitations: Does not run npm install or other dependency setup automatically
Does not handle complex merge conflicts or branch issues
Requires git to be installed and repository to be initialized
Does not support worktree deletion or cleanup

useCases.title: Isolated feature development
useCases.description: Create clean, isolated workspaces for working on feature branches without affecting your main development directory.

useCases.title: Review PR branches locally
useCases.description: Quickly check out pull request branches in isolated worktrees to test changes before merging.

useCases.title: Parallel experiment workspaces
useCases.description: Run multiple experiments or fixes simultaneously in separate worktrees for clean comparison.

promptTemplates.title: Basic worktree creation
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a simple feature branch worktree
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a git worktree for branch feature/my-new-feature

promptTemplates.title: From specific base
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a git worktree for branch hotfix/security-fix --from develop

promptTemplates.title: With branch convention
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating worktree with nested branch path
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a git worktree for branch feature/api/user-authentication

promptTemplates.title: After main work
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating worktree after completing other work
promptTemplates.prompt: I've finished my current task. Now create a worktree for bugfix/login-issue

bestPractices: Use meaningful branch names that clearly describe the work being done
Delete worktrees after merging to keep the worktrees directory organized
Keep .env files updated in the main repository for consistent environment setup
antiPatterns: Creating worktrees for branches that already exist locally without checking
Running the script from inside a worktree directory instead of the main repo
Modifying files in multiple worktrees that share the same .env without understanding implications

faq.question: What git versions support worktree?
faq.answer: Git worktree requires git version 2.5 or higher. Most modern git installations support this feature.

faq.question: How many worktrees can I create?
faq.answer: You can create as many worktrees as needed, but each requires sufficient disk space for the working copy.

faq.question: Does this work with GitHub CLI or other git tools?
faq.answer: Yes, this script works independently and can be used alongside GitHub CLI, git aliases, or other git tools.

faq.question: Is my .env file data safe?
faq.answer: The script only copies .env to the local worktree directory. No data is sent externally or shared.

faq.question: What if the worktree creation fails?
faq.answer: Check that git is installed, you have repository permissions, and the branch name is valid. The script exits with error details.

faq.question: How does this differ from cloning the repository?
faq.answer: Worktrees share the repository history with the main clone, using less disk space and enabling instant branch switching.
